methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Vuecason is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Dival Banerjee that has raised $3M+. The target operates within a sector represented by 9 private peers (including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics) and 1 public comparable.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method, using the $3M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement in the sector. This yields an implied valuation of $8M. Peer-median triangulation is not applicable: while 11 same-sector peers exist in the Robotics & Manufacturing universe, none satisfy narrow filtering criteria (same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier) at the ≥3-datapoint threshold required for statistical reliability. Relying on all-sector median would introduce unacceptable methodological noise given the heterogeneity of robotics subsectors and development stages.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $6M (low) to $9M (high), centered on $8M. This range reflects a conservative 15% confidence interval around the stage-progression result. Material uncertainties include the precise definition of "raised" (equity vs. total capital), burn trajectory and runway relative to next funding event, and technical validation status—factors not quantified in available data but critical to seed-stage robotics risk assessment. Any revision would require tightening the peer set or obtaining comparable transaction multiples from recent seed rounds in autonomous manufacturing.
